channel.title: NPR Topics: Culture
channel.description: Art and entertainment commentary plus interviews, book reviews, movie reviews, music reviews, comedy, and visual art. Subscribe to podcasts and follow trends in music, painting, art, architecture, photography, and design.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

item.title: 'Diaphanous' crowned the most beautiful English word
item.description: As part of a collaboration between the Poetry Society of America and Babbel, 1,500 poets and poetry lovers weighed in on the most beautiful word in the English language, crowning "diaphanous."
item.content:encoded: As part of a collaboration between the Poetry Society of America and Babbel, 1,500 poets and poetry lovers weighed in on the most beautiful word in the English language, crowning "diaphanous."
item.dc:creator: Kathryn Fink

item.title: To Detroiters, Motown is more than just music
item.description: The story of Motown Records is a quintessentially American story about the American Dream, civil rights and entrepreneurship — but it has a special significance in Detroit, where it all started.
item.content:encoded: The story of Motown Records is a quintessentially American story about the American Dream, civil rights and entrepreneurship — but it has a special significance in Detroit, where it all started.
item.dc:creator: Don Gonyea

item.title: 'Toy Story 5' surges into the digital age -- while also peering lovingly backward
item.description: In this delightful sequel, Jessie the cowgirl teams up with Buzz Lightyear and Woody to fend off the rise of digital devices, which are taking over the minds and attention spans of kids everywhere.
item.content:encoded:
In this delightful sequel, Jessie the cowgirl teams up with Buzz Lightyear and Woody to fend off the rise of digital devices, which are taking over the minds and attention spans of kids everywhere.

(Image credit: Disney)
item.dc:creator: Justin Chang

item.title: Comic Ali Siddiq makes peace with the past in 'My Father'
item.description: As a kid with an aching tooth, Siddiq remembers his dad treating it with cocaine he'd stashed in a Cool Whip container. Now Siddiq pays homage to his dad in the comedy special My Father.
item.content:encoded:
My Father.'/>

As a kid with an aching tooth, Siddiq remembers his dad treating it with cocaine he'd stashed in a Cool Whip container. Now Siddiq pays homage to his dad in the comedy special My Father.

(Image credit: David 'Odi' Wright)
item.dc:creator: Tonya Mosley

item.title: Hugh Jackman's tormented 'Robin Hood' faces a reckoning
item.description: Director Michael Sarnoski's film about the legendary hero who robs from the rich and gives to the poor is about the stories we tell ourselves.
item.content:encoded:
Director Michael Sarnoski's film about the legendary hero who robs from the rich and gives to the poor is about the stories we tell ourselves.

(Image credit: A24)
item.dc:creator: Bob Mondello

item.title: What — and who — will be at the Great American State Fair? Here's a primer
item.description: Several state governments have declined to participate in the 16-day event, though organizers say all U.S. states and territories will be represented by booths on the National Mall.
item.content:encoded:
Several state governments have declined to participate in the 16-day event, though organizers say all U.S. states and territories will be represented by booths on the National Mall.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Rachel Treisman

item.title: Reminiscent of a former era in American politics, the Obama Presidential Center opens
item.description: The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony featured celebrities and speeches from former President Barack Obama and former first lady Michelle Obama focused on their legacy.
item.content:encoded:
The Obama Presidential Center's grand opening ceremony featured celebrities and speeches from former President Barack Obama and former first lady Michelle Obama focused on their legacy.

(Image credit: Win McNamee)
item.dc:creator: Tamara Keith

item.title: For author Jane Yolen, no word was too big for a children's book
item.description: Yolen, who authored The Devil's Arithmetic and the picture book Owl Moon, was an author's author — known for gathering loved ones in her Massachusetts home and collaborating with her friends and family.
item.content:encoded:
Yolen, who authored The Devil's Arithmetic and the picture book Owl Moon, was an author's author — known for gathering loved ones in her Massachusetts home and collaborating with her friends and family.

(Image credit: Denver Post)
item.dc:creator: Chloee Weiner

item.title: Greetings from Maputo, Mozambique's capital, shaped by a modernist architecture
item.description: An impromptu tour of Mozambique's capital city reveals a unique imprint left by architect Amâncio "Pancho" Guedes.
item.content:encoded: An impromptu tour of Mozambique's capital city reveals a unique imprint left by architect Amâncio "Pancho" Guedes.
item.dc:creator: Matt Ozug

item.title: On a flight home, a stranger helped her understand what came next
item.description: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.
item.content:encoded: Rebecca Simonitsch had just learned she might be a candidate for brain surgery. The man seated beside her on the flight home pulled out a notebook to explain what lay ahead.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kwerel
